stories.headline: Meta to Manufacture Its Own AI Chip From September, Racing Toward 14 Gigawatts of Compute
stories.key_takeaway: Meta will begin manufacturing its in-house 'Iris' AI chip in September to more than double its computing capacity to 14 gigawatts by 2027 and cut its dependence on Nvidia.

pull_quote.text: We're building multiple more titan clusters as well. Just one of these covers a significant part of the footprint of Manhattan.

stories.article_excerpt: Meta Platforms is done waiting in line for someone else's silicon. According to an internal memo reviewed by Reuters, the company...

stories.headline: Ollama Raises $65 Million Series B Led by Theory Ventures to Scale Local AI
stories.key_takeaway: Ollama, the free local-AI runtime now used by 8.9 million developers, raised a $65M Series B led by Theory Ventures on a bet that the layer where open models run is becoming one of the most valuable positions in software.

pull_quote.text: As open models close the gap for most real work, the platform where AI runs becomes one of the most valuable positions in software.

stories.article_excerpt: The tool that lets developers run open AI models on their own laptops just became one of the best-funded bets on where inference lives.

stories.headline: Prime Intellect Raises $130 Million Series A Led by Radical Ventures for Decentralized AI Training
stories.key_takeaway: Prime Intellect raised a $130M Series A led by Radical Ventures at a roughly $1B valuation, backing its bet that frontier AI can be trained on globally pooled, decentralized GPUs rather than inside centralized hyperscaler clusters.

pull_quote.text: It shouldn't just be a few nerds in a glass tower in San Francisco that have the capability to train AI models. It should be every enterprise, every nation state.

stories.article_excerpt: Prime Intellect, the startup arguing that frontier AI need not be built inside a handful of hyperscaler-funded labs, now has $130 million and a unicorn valuation to prove it.

stories.headline: Norm AI Raises $120 Million Series C Led by Khosla Ventures to Automate Regulatory Compliance
stories.key_takeaway: Norm Ai raised a $120M Series C at a $1.2B valuation led by Khosla Ventures, betting that turning regulations into machine-readable AI agents makes compliance the trust wedge for agentic AI in the enterprise.

pull_quote.text: AI will not transform regulated work until institutions trust it, and that trust is the hardest thing to earn in this market. The most demanding buyers of legal services in the world already rely on Norm Ai.

stories.article_excerpt: On July 7, Norm Ai's audacious idea graduated into unicorn territory: a $120 million Series C at a $1.2 billion valuation, led by Khosla Ventures.

stories.headline: Rackspace Bets Its Future on the Enterprise AI Stack With a New Capital Raise
stories.key_takeaway: Rackspace is pivoting from legacy hosting to operating the full governed enterprise AI stack, funding the shift with a $250M equity raise even as it cut 2026 guidance by $150M and saw shares fall ~25%.

pull_quote.text: Today's capital raise announcement is meaningful because it will enable us to expedite our AI Enterprise strategy and unlock a meaningful revenue and EBITDA growth vector for Rackspace, starting in 2027.

stories.article_excerpt: Rackspace Technology spent a quarter-century renting out servers. On July 9, the company told investors it now intends to sell the entire enterprise AI stack.

stories.headline: Meta's Muse Spark 1.1 Is a Cheap, Agent-Native Multimodal Model Built for Orchestration
stories.key_takeaway: Meta's first paid frontier model, Muse Spark 1.1, undercuts Anthropic and OpenAI at $1.25/$4.25 per million tokens and specializes in agent orchestration, shipping with an API that speaks both OpenAI and Anthropic formats.

pull_quote.text: Today we're releasing Muse Spark 1.1 — a strong agentic and coding model at a very low price.

stories.article_excerpt: Late on the night of July 9, 2026, Mark Zuckerberg did something he had not done in three years: he posted on X. The occasion was Muse Spark 1.1.

stories.headline: OpenAI Launches ChatGPT Work, Folding Coding and Productivity Into One AI Workspace
stories.key_takeaway: ChatGPT Work productizes OpenAI's agent layer into a single workspace uniting chat, Codex coding, and productivity, delivering finished spreadsheets, slides, docs, and web apps in direct competition with Microsoft Copilot and Google Workspace.

pull_quote.text: That helped us identify and hand off seven figures in pipeline every month to sales.

stories.article_excerpt: For three years, 'using AI at work' meant a chat window sitting beside the work. On July 9, OpenAI tried to collapse that gap with ChatGPT Work.

stories.headline: NVIDIA's Nemotron 3 Ultra Undercuts Closed Models on Agent Orchestration
stories.key_takeaway: NVIDIA's open-weight Nemotron 3 Ultra, a 550B/55B-active MoE model, matched top closed models on LangChain's Deep Agents enterprise benchmark at roughly 10x lower cost, signaling NVIDIA is now competing on the model layer, not just GPUs.

pull_quote.text: The way to build better agents is to keep improving the system around the model. Our work with NVIDIA shows that enterprises can get strong performance from an open stack while keeping control over the agent systems they are building.

stories.article_excerpt: On July 8, NVIDIA published benchmark results showing its open-weight Nemotron 3 Ultra matched the best closed models on enterprise agent tasks at one-tenth the cost.

stories.headline: KAIST's VOTP Teaches Robots Human Judgment From Just a Handful of Videos
stories.key_takeaway: KAIST's VOTP uses optimal transport over video-model embeddings to auto-generate preference labels, letting robots learn human judgment from a handful of videos instead of tens of thousands of hand-labeled comparisons — an ICML 2026 Oral.

pull_quote.text: The core of physical AI is making machines understand human intentions and choose the correct actions. Since VOTP can learn human judgment criteria with only a small number of videos, it is a core technology that will accelerate the era of robots making human-like judgments.
pull_quote.role: Professor, KAIST School of Electrical Engineering

stories.article_excerpt: Teaching a robot what 'good' looks like has long been reinforcement learning's most expensive chore. KAIST says it can now get much of that judgment from a handful of videos.

stories.headline: Nature Medicine Studies Show Agentic AI Can Aid Patient Care — But Isn't Clinic-Ready
stories.key_takeaway: Two agentic AI systems, Dresden's MIRA and Google's AMIE, matched or beat physicians on diagnosis and management in simulated cases, but their creators and Nature's editors agree neither is ready for real-world clinical use.

pull_quote.text: These systems can support and relieve medical professionals by taking over routine tasks, but ultimate responsibility will always remain with the physicians.
pull_quote.role: MIRA co-developer, TUD Dresden University of Technology

stories.article_excerpt: Two experimental AI agents can take a history, order tests, weigh results and draft a plan — and in simulated cases they matched or beat physicians. But neither is ready to touch a real patient.

stories.headline: Newspapers Ask a Federal Court to Sanction OpenAI Over Copyright Discovery
stories.key_takeaway: Newspapers led by The New York Times asked the SDNY court to sanction OpenAI, alleging it spent two years falsely claiming it could not search its models and ChatGPT logs for copyrighted journalism while internally running exactly those searches.

pull_quote.text: If OpenAI genuinely believed that copying our clients' journalism was fair and legal, it wouldn't have hid the truth about having done it.
pull_quote.role: Lead counsel for the news plaintiffs

stories.article_excerpt: A coalition of newspapers led by The New York Times has asked a Manhattan federal court to punish OpenAI over what it calls hidden evidence in the copyright case.

stories.headline: A Judge Freezes Colorado's Landmark AI Bias Law After a Challenge From Musk's xAI
stories.key_takeaway: A federal magistrate judge stayed enforcement of Colorado's SB 24-205 after xAI sued it as unconstitutional and the DOJ intervened, marking the opening move of the administration's strategy to preempt state AI laws through litigation.

pull_quote.text: coerce our nation's technological innovators into producing harmful products that advance a radical, far left worldview at odds with the Constitution
pull_quote.role: Assistant Attorney General, U.S. Department of Justice

stories.article_excerpt: Colorado spent two years building the country's most ambitious law against discriminatory AI. It took Elon Musk's xAI barely two weeks to help freeze it.
